description:
Comprehensive production-hardening effort for review findings from March 2026.

Scope
- Eliminate protocol corruption risks from concurrent access and error-path desync.
- Harden wire decoder and auth parsing against malformed/untrusted input.
- Close operational safety gaps (TLS defaults, pool capacity leaks, timeouts/cancel).
- Add regression tests for every fixed failure mode.

Execution phases
1) Critical protocol safety: serialized connection operations + stream/COPY resync + decoder count/length validation.
2) High-priority robustness: pool permit leak, notification FIFO correctness, stream cleanup guarantees, secure TLS defaults, decoder buffer caps.
3) Medium hardening: strict SCRAM parsing + username escaping, cancel API and timeout behavior.
4) Validation and release gate: targeted unit tests + integration rerun against live Postgres.

Definition of done
- All linked child issues closed with tests.
- `moon check --target native` green.
- Targeted unit suites green.
- Integration suite green against running PostgreSQL.
title: Production hardening pass for concurrency/protocol/security review

description: Fix review finding #1 by enforcing single in-flight protocol operation per Client/Connection across query, prepared statements, stream, COPY, and notification wait APIs. Add an internal operation guard (acquire/release with error-safe release), ensure stream/copy long-lived flows hold exclusivity correctly, and add concurrency regression tests that previously corrupted protocol flow.
notes: Implemented per-connection operation_guard semaphore and routed query/prepare/bind_execute/copy_in/copy_out/wait_for_notification/prepared close/stream lifecycle through serialized access. Stream now holds guard across active portal lifetime and releases deterministically on exhaustion/error/close. Need dedicated concurrency integration validation before closing.
title: Serialize per-client protocol operations to prevent concurrent socket/decoder interleaving

description: Address review finding #10 by adding a cancel API using backend PID/secret and ensuring read-timeout behavior is documented/tested for hung operations. Cover both user-initiated cancellation and timeout ergonomics in client-facing APIs.
title: Add query cancellation pathway and tighten timeout behavior for hung operations

description: After fixes land, run moon check/test suites and integration tests against a live Postgres instance. Produce a go/no-go checklist covering concurrency safety, protocol resync, decoder hardening, TLS defaults, SCRAM, and timeout/cancel behavior.
title: Run full hardening regression matrix and release readiness checklist

description: Fix review finding #2 by ensuring every error path in RowStream and COPY drains protocol state back to ReadyForQuery before surfacing errors. Include callback-error handling, server ErrorResponse handling, and explicit Sync/drain rules for extended-protocol flows.
title: Guarantee protocol resynchronization to ReadyForQuery on stream/COPY error paths

description: Fix review finding #3 by validating signed/unsigned protocol counts and lengths before allocation/iteration. Cover DataRow column counts and lengths, RowDescription field count, ParameterDescription count, Copy response count, and NegotiateProtocolVersion option count. Reject malformed negatives and overflows safely.
title: Harden decoder against negative/invalid wire lengths and counts

description: Fix review finding #7 by bounding buffered bytes and maximum frame size for partial/incomplete protocol frames. Ensure malformed oversized frames are rejected without unbounded growth, and add regression tests for repeated truncated oversized messages.
title: Cap decoder buffering for incomplete frames to prevent memory pressure attacks

close_reason: Fixed permit release on connect failure and added pool_wbtest regression
description: Fix review finding #4: ensure Pool::acquire always releases acquired semaphore permit if creating/replacing a client fails. Add regression coverage proving capacity is preserved after repeated failed connect attempts.
title: Fix pool semaphore permit leak when connect fails after acquire

close_reason: Switched notification dequeue to FIFO and made wait_for_notification batch-safe via enqueue+post-batch dequeue
description: Fix review finding #5 by replacing LIFO pop behavior and eliminating early-return batch drops in wait_for_notification. Guarantee FIFO ordering for queued notifications and preserve all notifications parsed in a receive batch.
title: Make notification handling FIFO and batch-safe

description: Fix review finding #6 by changing Config::new default ssl_mode from Disable to a secure default and updating docs/tests/examples accordingly. Preserve explicit opt-out via ssl_mode=Disable for local/plain deployments.
title: Switch default TLS mode to secure-by-default and document opt-out

description: Fix review finding #8 by guaranteeing statement/portal/transaction cleanup when stream setup or RowStream::for_each callback fails. Add defensive close/rollback behavior and tests for exception paths.
title: Ensure stream resources are always cleaned up on callback/setup exceptions

description: Fix review finding #9 by enforcing strict iteration parsing bounds, rejecting malformed server-first/server-final fields, and escaping username in SCRAM client-first per RFC 5802 (= becomes =3D, comma becomes =2C). Add unit tests for malicious and invalid inputs.
title: Tighten SCRAM parsing and username escaping for RFC-compliant auth hardening
